value.name: Residual Learning Framework
value.description: Reformulates the underlying mapping H(x) as F(x) + x, where stacked nonlinear layers learn the residual function F(x) = H(x) - x instead of the unreferenced mapping directly. This allows identity mappings to be approached by driving residuals to zero.
value.novelty_vs_prior: Prior work focused on fitting unreferenced mappings or used gated shortcuts (highway networks). This method uses parameter-free identity shortcuts to explicitly learn residuals, ensuring all information is passed through while adding residual corrections.

value.name: Identity Shortcut Connections
value.description: Shortcut connections that skip one or more layers and perform identity mapping, adding their output to the stacked layers' output via element-wise addition. They introduce no extra parameters or computational complexity.
value.novelty_vs_prior: While shortcut connections existed previously (e.g., in Inception or auxiliary classifiers), this paper specifically adopts parameter-free identity shortcuts for residual learning, contrasting with highway networks which use learned gating functions.

value.name: Bottleneck Building Block
value.description: A 3-layer building block for deeper ResNets (50+ layers) consisting of 1x1, 3x3, and 1x1 convolutions. The 1x1 layers reduce and restore dimensions, creating a bottleneck with smaller input/output dimensions for the 3x3 layer to improve efficiency.
value.novelty_vs_prior: Designed specifically for ResNets to enable extreme depth (152 layers) while maintaining lower complexity than VGG nets. It leverages identity shortcuts to avoid doubling model size/complexity that projection shortcuts would cause.

value.name: Batch Normalization
value.description: Normalization technique applied right after each convolution and before activation to stabilize and accelerate training.
value.novelty_vs_prior: Introduced by Ioffe and Szegedy (2015), used here as a standard component in the training pipeline following prior practices.
